items.q: What is the difference between CAD and CAM?
items.correct: CAD creates digital designs; CAM uses those designs to control manufacturing machines.
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: CAD is for cars, CAM is for cameras.
items.why: Students treat the terms as synonymous because they often appear together. CAD = drawing/modelling software; CAM = machine control (CNC routers, 3D printers) - typically the second stage.

items.q: What does 'Rethink' mean in the 6 Rs?
items.correct: Questioning whether a product is necessary or could be designed differently for sustainability.
items.misconception: Trying a design again if it does not work first time.
items.why: Students apply 'rethink' to design iteration. In sustainability, it means challenging the NEED for a product altogether, or fundamentally rethinking how it's made - a more radical step than reducing materials.

items.q: What gives carbon fibre composite its strength?
items.correct: The fibres provide tensile strength; the resin matrix holds them in place and transfers loads.
items.misconception: The carbon fibres alone - the resin is just glue.
items.why: Students dismiss the matrix as filler. In reality the matrix is essential: it transfers loads between fibres and protects them. Loose carbon fibres without matrix have very little structural strength.

items.q: Which converts rotary to reciprocating motion?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Cam. “Gear” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Output force / input force. “Distance/time” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many classes of lever are there?
items.why: The real figure is 3 classes. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
